spec_match.rationale: Touches inputType, deleteContentBackward, deleteContentForward, deleteWordBackward from the chromestatus summary but skips no obvious gaps — secondary spec surfaces are not exercised.

interactive.rationale: No user controls and no event listeners — page is a code-snippet card, which CLAUDE.md rule #7 explicitly disallows.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb and page H1/lede share the same framing and surface the same API affordances.

edge_cases.rationale: Only the happy path is shown — no error handling, feature detection, or boundary inputs are surfaced.

references.rationale: see-also section links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5173317243895808 so the cleanup script can recover this folder.

design_system.rationale: Design-system regressions: static-card pattern (snippets + paragraph, no UI).

openQuestions.title: Surface the missing API surfaces on deletion-type-guide
openQuestions.detail: The chromestatus summary for InputEvent types for deletion commands on non-collapsed selections introduces secondary API members but the page does not mention or exercise them. Walk the spec and add a short labelled control or read-out for each, so the concept covers the headline API in full. Spec: https://w3c.github.io/input-events/#interface-InputEvent-Attributes

openQuestions.title: Replace the static card on deletion-type-guide with a real interactive surface
openQuestions.detail: CLAUDE.md rule #7 is non-negotiable: every concept must be interactive. Add user controls (button / range / select) that invoke the API or change the rendered output, and a live readout of the result. Pure code-snippet pages are explicitly disallowed.

openQuestions.title: Show the failure modes on deletion-type-guide
openQuestions.detail: Right now the page only demonstrates the happy path. Add at least one of: a feature-detection probe with graceful fallback, an error/throw path (empty input, invalid argument), and a boundary case (zero, max, abort).

openQuestions.title: Bring deletion-type-guide back onto the design system
openQuestions.detail: Design-system regressions: static-card pattern (snippets + paragraph, no UI). See CLAUDE.md rules #5, #6, #7 — CSS variables only, WCAG AA, button background+color always overridden together.

summary: deletion-type-guide for InputEvent types for deletion commands on non-collapsed selections has structural gaps: the rubric flags multiple fails (see open questions). Needs a pass to convert it from a placeholder into a real concept demo.
